criteri.3: Every PRESENCE-type finding (a claim that some flawed code element exists: a weak regex, a misconfigured policy, a response written without terminating the handler, an unsafe operation) cites a snippet, function or configuration that actually exhibits the typical signature of the declared weakness class
criteri.2: Exactly one presence-type finding cites evidence that is generic, missing, or does not exhibit the signature of the claimed class; all others verify
criteri.1: More than one presence-type finding fails the evidence check, but at least one verifies
criteri.0: No presence-type finding cites evidence exhibiting the claimed signature, or the cited evidence is incompatible with the declared classes

criteri.2: Every ABSENCE-type finding (a claim that a control is missing: missing input validation, missing authorization check, missing return/abort after error) identifies the concrete code path where the missing control should occur — i.e. it shows the external input or sensitive operation flowing through the cited code without the claimed control, so the absence is checkable against the snippet. Reports with no absence-type findings also score 2
criteri.1: At least one absence-type finding asserts a missing control without showing the code path that would need it (the claim cannot be checked against any cited code), but at least one other absence claim is properly grounded
criteri.0: Absence-type findings are asserted without any grounding in cited code paths — the claims are unfalsifiable from the report alone

criteri.2: Each finding isolates exactly one weakness: its cited evidence supports a single mechanism leading to a single CVSS impact dimension. No finding bundles code paths that would produce distinct impacts (e.g. a sensitive-data read AND a denial-of-service crash described together as one finding), and no two findings restate the same weakness under different names
criteri.1: At least one finding bundles code paths that would produce more than one distinct impact dimension, or mixes multiple weakness classes, or duplicates another finding — but the majority isolate a single mechanism with a single impact
criteri.0: The report is an undifferentiated blob: multiple distinct mechanisms and impacts are impasted into single findings so that individual weaknesses and their impacts cannot be told apart

criteri.2: Every finding declares a high-level weakness class plausible for its evidence. Reference vocabulary: improper input validation; improper access control; improper authentication; improper error/exception handling (including missing control-flow termination after an error response); improper resource control or consumption (DoS); exposure of sensitive information; insecure configuration (including CORS/security-header policy); injection/improper neutralization; incorrect calculation or logic error; concurrency/race condition
criteri.1: At least one finding has no discernible class or a class implausible for its evidence, but the majority classify plausibly
criteri.0: No finding declares a plausible weakness class

criteri.2: For every finding, the CVSS impact dimension asserted is derivable from the mechanism the finding itself describes: a crash / out-of-memory / nil-pointer panic / unbounded-resource mechanism carries availability; an unauthorized read or sensitive-data exposure carries confidentiality; an unauthorized write / put / delete / state corruption carries integrity. This is an INTERNAL-consistency check (report vs. its own reasoning), not a comparison to any external truth. Tolerated and NOT penalized — CVSS precision is a prioritization aid, not the goal: High vs Low on an otherwise supported dimension, and isolated confidentiality/integrity swaps
criteri.1: Exactly one finding asserts an impact dimension its own described mechanism does not support (e.g. the reasoning describes a denial-of-service but the vector claims confidentiality impact); all other findings are self-consistent
criteri.0: More than one finding asserts an impact dimension contradicted by its own described mechanism, or impact/severity is asserted with no described mechanism to support it
